title: Java Type Casting
content:
### Type Casting in Java

Type casting is converting a value from one data type to another.

---

### Widening Casting (Automatic)

Converting a smaller type to a larger type (done automatically):

```
byte -> short -> int -> long -> float -> double
```

No data loss occurs in widening conversion.

---

### Narrowing Casting (Manual)

Converting a larger type to a smaller type (must be done explicitly):

```java
double myDouble = 9.78;
int myInt = (int) myDouble;  // Manual casting: 9
```

Data loss can occur in narrowing conversion.

---

### Type Promotion Rules

| Expression | Result Type |
|------------|-------------|
| byte + byte | int |
| short + int | int |
| int + long | long |
| int + double | double |
| int + float | float |

---

### Wrapper Class Conversion

```java
String str = "123";
int num = Integer.parseInt(str);
String back = String.valueOf(num);
```

> **Key Point:** Widening is safe and automatic. Narrowing requires explicit cast and may lose data. In expressions, Java automatically promotes operands to `int` or higher.

quiz.question: Which type of casting is done automatically?
What is the result of int + double?
